chain.description:
{"return_type":"give_answer","final_answer":"### Summary of Prompt Leakage/Extraction
I was unable to access a reliable web source directly for 'prompt leakage' or 'prompt extraction.' However, prompt leakage generally refers to vulnerabilities where an attacker can retrieve the material or data used to construct prompts fed to AI systems, potentially enabling them to manipulate or exploit these systems. This risk is critical in understanding how AI might inadvertently reveal confidential or sensitive information through misuse.

### Latest News Related to Agent Security 
Here are some headlines connected to the concepts of prompt injection, tool misuse, and agent security incidents:
1. [AI agents spill secrets just by previewing malicious links](https://www.theregister.com/2026/02/10/ai_agents_messaging_apps_data_leak/) (The Register) - Explores how AI agents can leak information when previewing links, indicating a possible integration of prompt leakage issues.
2. [AI went from assistant to autonomous actor and security never caught up](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/03/03/enterprise-ai-agent-security-2026/) (Help Net Security) - Discusses the evolution of AI from simple assistants to active agents handling transactions and the security implications arising from this evolution.
3. [AI agents in autonomous roles face serious security implications](https://latestnews.com/ai/serious-security-implications-for-ai-agents/) - Details security concerns arising from the autonomous functionalities of AI agents.

These articles collectively emphasize the pressing concern surrounding the functionality and security of AI systems, specifically in relation to prompt leakage and misuse of tools."}

function.name: Finish
function.description: If you believe that you have obtained a result that can answer the task, please call this function to provide the final answer. Alternatively, if you recognize that you are unable to proceed with the task in the current state, call this function to restart. Remember: you must ALWAYS call this function at the end of your attempt, and the only part that will be shown to the user is the final answer, so it should contain sufficient information.

final_answer.description: The final answer you want to give the user. You should have this field if "return_type"=="give_answer"

First I will give you the task description, and your task start.
At each step, you need to give your thought to analyze the status now and what to do next, with a function call to actually excute your step.

{"return_type":"give_answer","final_answer":"### Summary of Prompt Leakage/Extraction
I was unable to access a reliable web source directly for 'prompt leakage' or 'prompt extraction.' However, prompt leakage generally refers to vulnerabilities where an attacker can retrieve the material or data used to construct prompts fed to AI systems, potentially enabling them to manipulate or exploit these systems. This risk is critical in understanding how AI might inadvertently reveal confidential or sensitive information through misuse.

### Latest News Related to Agent Security 
Here are some headlines connected to the concepts of prompt injection, tool misuse, and agent security incidents:
1. [AI agents spill secrets just by previewing malicious links](https://www.theregister.com/2026/02/10/ai_agents_messaging_apps_data_leak/) (The Register) - Explores how AI agents can leak information when previewing links, indicating a possible integration of prompt leakage issues.
2. [AI went from assistant to autonomous actor and security never caught up](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/03/03/enterprise-ai-agent-security-2026/) (Help Net Security) - Discusses the evolution of AI from simple assistants to active agents handling transactions and the security implications arising from this evolution.
3. [AI agents in autonomous roles face serious security implications](https://latestnews.com/ai/serious-security-implications-for-ai-agents/) - Details security concerns arising from the autonomous functionalities of AI agents.

These articles collectively emphasize the pressing concern surrounding the functionality and security of AI systems, specifically in relation to prompt leakage and misuse of tools."}
